reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes all parts of the informal claim. The hypotheses `Set.Countable S` and `S.Infinite` together mean S is countably infinite, and `span K S = ⊤` says S spans V. `[Field K] [Module K V]` is the standard encoding of "V is a K-vector space." The conclusion `Countable ι` in Mathlib means "at most countable" (finite or countably infinite), matching the informal conclusion. The use of a linearly independent family `R : ι → V` rather than a subset is equivalent: `LinearIndependent K R` forces R to be injective, so ι has the same cardinality as the linearly independent image set, making "every such ι is countable" equivalent to "every linearly independent subset is at most countable."
